rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Implementation-level contracts should only reference visible outputs and declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope when writing AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
Avoid using AADL property names as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: assume/guarantee syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
AGREE contract generation and fusion rules
